notes.introduction: A topical lesson titled 'What About Dinosaurs?' preached as part of an ongoing series through Genesis, pausing after chapter 19. Drawing primarily from Genesis 1:24-25 and Job 40:15-24, the sermon addresses common evolutionary claims about dinosaurs and offers biblical and historical responses from a creationist perspective.
outline: I. Common Teaching About Dinosaurs: three standard evolutionary claims examined, including the 165-million-year timeline, the asteroid extinction event, and the assertion that humans and dinosaurs never coexisted
II. Why the Word 'Dinosaur' Does Not Appear in the Bible: the term was coined by Sir Richard Owen in 1841 and therefore postdates the biblical text
III. Where Did Dinosaurs Come From: God created all land creatures on day six (Genesis 1:24-25), with some possibly created on day five, rejecting evolutionary origin from other reptiles
IV. Did Humans and Dinosaurs Coexist: Genesis 1:26-28 places man's creation on the same day as land animals, and Job 40 describes behemoth as a creature God made alongside man

V. What Happened to the Dinosaurs and Why Are There So Many Fossils: the global flood of Genesis 6-8 killed those not on the ark, producing rapid fossilization, while Noah brought dinosaurs onto and off the ark

VI. Historical Evidence of Post-Flood Dinosaurs: references to Alexander the Great's accounts, Marco Polo's travels, the Narmer Palette, a Stegosaurus carving on a Cambodian temple, the Chinese Zodiac dragon, and a 2017 fossil discovery with preserved stomach contents
